info.title: Run a Call External API
info.description: > A bearer-authenticated REST API for pushing data into and pulling data out of **[Run a Call](https://runacall.com)** — the field service platform built for small-to-mid HVAC companies. Migrate from your old CRM, sync with accounting tools, fire Zapier / Make / n8n flows on real-time events, or wire your own scripts. Same surface internally — no second-class endpoints. ## ⚡ Quick start Three lines to your first request. Replace the token with one from **[Settings → Integrations → API](https://app.runacall.com/settings/integrations/api)** in your dashboard. ```bash curl https://api.runacall.com/v1/customers \ -H "Authorization: Bearer rkc_live_..." ``` That's it. Bearer header, JSON in, JSON out. --- ## 🔑 Authentication Every request needs an `Authorization: Bearer rkc_live_...` header. | Scope | Grants | |---|---| | `read` | All `GET` endpoints | | `write` | `POST` and `PATCH` on customers / properties / equipment / jobs / memberships | Tokens are **SHA-256 hashed at rest**. The raw value is shown to the org owner exactly once at creation — we cannot retrieve it later. Rotate at any time from the same settings page; the old token works for a 24-hour grace window so deploys don't break. --- ## 🛡️ Tenant isolation Every request is scoped to the organization that owns the bearer token. - Cross-org reads return **404 (not 403)** to mask existence - No `admin` scope, no DELETE endpoints, no destructive verbs - Soft-delete (`is_deleted = true`) is a UI-only operation - RLS + service-role org-filter run independently — defense in depth --- ## 🚦 Rate limits | Layer | Limit | Window | |---|---|---| | Per key — sustained | 60 req | 1 min rolling | | Per key — burst | 120 req | 10 s rolling | | Per org — all keys combined | 300 req | 1 min rolling | | Concurrent in-flight per key | 5 | — | | Per-IP — unauthed paths | 10 req | 1 min rolling | Exceeding any layer returns **`429 Too Many Requests`** with a `Retry-After` header. Founders can raise a known integrator's limit via per-key override — email **[admin@runacall.com](mailto:admin@runacall.com)**. --- ## 🔁 Idempotency — upsert by `external_id` Every writable resource (customers, properties, equipment, jobs, memberships) accepts `external_id` + `external_source`. POSTing the same pair twice **upserts the existing row** — returns 200 with the same UUID instead of 201 with a duplicate. ```bash # First call — creates curl -X POST https://api.runacall.com/v1/customers \ -H "Authorization: Bearer rkc_live_..." \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"external_id":"crm-acme-001","external_source":"servicetitan","first_name":"Jenny","last_name":"Rosen"}' # → 201 Created, id: 497f6eca-6276-4993-bfeb-53cbbbba6f08 # Same call again — upserts, same id curl -X POST https://api.runacall.com/v1/customers \ -H "Authorization: Bearer rkc_live_..." \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"external_id":"crm-acme-001","external_source":"servicetitan","first_name":"Jenny","last_name":"Rosen"}' # → 200 OK, id: 497f6eca-6276-4993-bfeb-53cbbbba6f08 ``` Safe to retry. Safe to script bulk imports against. Network blip? Re-fire — you'll never end up with duplicates. --- ## 📄 Pagination Cursor-based, never offset. Pass back the `next_cursor` from a response as `?starting_after=` to fetch the next page. ```json { "data": [ /* up to 100 items */ ], "has_more": true, "next_cursor": "MjAyNi0wNS0wN1QwODo1OTowMi4xMjIzNDQrMDA6MDB8MzZhNTlmMDQ" } ``` Default page size 50, hard cap 100. There's no way to ask for "all rows" in a single call — keep your loop honest. --- ## ❌ Errors Every error response is the same envelope: ```json { "error": { "code": "invalid_request", "message": "external_id is required when upserting", "doc_url": "https://docs.runacall.com/#errors-invalid_request", "request_id": "req_a93a7817f5a8485d80204becea" } } ``` Quote **`request_id`** when reaching out for support — it correlates to our audit log. ### `invalid_request` — 400 / 422 Body or query parameter validation failed. The `message` field names the specific issue. Common causes: missing required field, unknown field (`.strict()` rejects extras), `external_id` without `external_source`, FK reference not found in this organization, invalid job status transition. ### `unauthorized` — 401 Missing or invalid bearer token. Check the `Authorization` header format: `Bearer rkc_live_...`. If you rotated the token recently, the old value works for 24 hours — verify you're using the right one. ### `forbidden` — 403 Your token lacks the required scope. `POST` and `PATCH` endpoints require the `write` scope; everything else needs `read`. Issue a new key with the right scopes from the Settings page. ### `not_found` — 404 The resource doesn't exist, OR it belongs to another organization. We mask cross-org existence to prevent enumeration attacks — there's no way to tell from outside which case it is. ### `conflict` — 409 Concurrent upsert race lost. Two POSTs hit the same `(external_id, external_source)` at once; only one won the unique-index race. **Retry the same payload** — your second attempt will hit the existing row via the upsert path and return 200. ### `rate_limited` — 429 One of the rate-limit layers above kicked in. The response includes a `Retry-After` header (seconds). Back off and retry. ### `internal_error` — 500 A bug on our end. Quote the `request_id` when emailing **admin@runacall.com** and we'll investigate. We log every 5xx response with full context. --- ## 🪝 Webhooks Push real-time events to your own endpoints instead of polling. Subscribe a URL via **[Settings → Integrations → Webhooks](https://app.runacall.com/settings/integrations/webhooks)**. **15 canonical events** across customers, jobs, estimates, invoices, payments, voice calls, and memberships. Every delivery is **HMAC-SHA256 signed** over `\${ts}.\${raw_body}` — header: `Run-A-Call-Signature: t=,v1=`. Verify the timestamp is within 5 minutes (replay protection), then verify the signature. Failed deliveries retry 3x with exponential backoff. After **10 consecutive failures** the subscription auto-pauses and we email the org owner — resume with one click. See the **Webhooks** section in the sidebar for the full event catalog + payload shapes. --- ## 📦 What you can build A few patterns the API was designed for: - **One-shot migration from your old CRM** — script a pull from ServiceTitan / Housecall Pro / a spreadsheet → POST against `/customers` + `/properties` + `/equipment` with your own `external_id`. Re-run safely; idempotent. - **Accounting sync** — subscribe to `invoice.paid` + `payment.received` webhooks → push into QuickBooks / Xero / your ERP. - **Zapier / Make / n8n automation** — webhook triggers fan out to Slack, Notion, Google Sheets, anywhere. - **Custom analytics** — `GET /voice-calls/{id}` returns transcripts. No other HVAC platform exposes this. Need an integration we haven't documented yet? Email **[admin@runacall.com](mailto:admin@runacall.com)** with what you're trying to do and we'll wire it.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description:
Outbound HTTP webhook deliveries from Run a Call to subscriber URLs.

**Authentication.** Every POST carries `Run-A-Call-Signature: t=<unix>,v1=<hex>`.
The signed string is `${ts}.${raw_body}`. Verify with HMAC-SHA256 against the
subscription secret. Reject if `Math.abs(now - t) > 5 minutes` to defeat
replay attacks.

**Deduplication.** Each delivery's `event.id` (header
`Run-A-Call-Event-Id`, also in the JSON body) is stable across retries
of the same logical event. Store the last N event ids you've processed
and dedupe before applying business logic.

**Retry policy.** Subscriber returns 2xx within 10 seconds → success.
Anything else (non-2xx, timeout, network error) is retried up to 3 total
attempts with default Inngest backoff. After 10 consecutive terminal
failures, the subscription is auto-paused and the org owner is emailed.
Resume from /settings/integrations/webhooks/{id}.

**Concurrency.** Run a Call caps fan-out at 100 matching subscriptions
per single internal event and concurrent deliveries at 50 in-flight per
function — beyond that, the system bails rather than fan out unbounded.

bearerAuth.description: Bearer token issued from /settings/integrations/api. Format: `rkc_live_<32 base62 chars>`. Hashed at rest; the raw token is shown to the org owner exactly once at creation.

sequence.description: 1-based ordinal within the parent job. Day 1 = sequence 1, Day 2 = sequence 2, etc.

status.description: Forward-only state machine. `wrap` = end-of-day on a multi-day install (the appointment is done for today, but the install isn't complete). Only the final-day appointment moves to `complete`.

customer_notified_at.description: Set when the on-the-way SMS fires (Lead-only action).

examples: Customer is home all day. Side gate code 4421.

assignments.description: Crew assigned to this appointment. Ordered Lead-first, then helpers alphabetical by tech_id.

tech_id.description: Profile id of the tech assigned to this appointment.

role.description: Exactly one Lead per appointment (DB partial unique index). Lead owns all customer-facing actions; helpers own their own clock state only.

scheduled_start.description: Optional per-tech window override. When null, falls back to the appointment-level scheduled_start.

tech_state.description: Independent per-tech state. Driven by the mobile app — not mutable via the External API in v1 (clock-in is a tech-on-device flow).

assigned_tech_id.description: Lead of the next-incomplete appointment (trigger-maintained denormalization from `appointments` → `appointment_assignments`). Preserves backward compatibility for integrators that consumed the legacy 1-tech-per-job shape.

examples: AC not cooling — possibly low refrigerant. Customer says it ran fine yesterday.
R-410A leak at evaporator coil. ~0.5 lb low.
Replaced evaporator coil. Recharged refrigerant to spec. Pressure-tested.

is_multi_day.description: True when this job has more than one appointment row.

total_days.description: Total appointment count (>= 1). 1 for service calls, 2+ for multi-day installs.

is_multi_tech.description: True when any appointment of this job has more than one assignment. DERIVED at wire-build time — accurate only when `appointments` are embedded (always on detail; opt-in via `?include=appointments` on list). When appointments are not embedded, this field defaults to `false` — set the query parameter if you need exact multi-tech detection on list responses.

appointments.description: Embedded appointments[]. Always present on `GET /api/v1/jobs/{id}` (detail). Present on list responses ONLY when `?include=appointments` is set. Omitted otherwise.

membership_id.description: Phase 20 (M0.e): the customer's membership at job creation time, or `null` for non-member jobs. Maintained at write time by job-creation paths (M0.f); historical jobs backfilled by migration 185 using a best-effort match (most recent `membership.start_date <= job.created_at`).

coverage_type.description: Phase 20 (M0.e): coverage classification per the memberships plan §2 decision #13. `membership_covered` = cron-scheduled tune-up (included scope $0). `membership_billable` = repair / add-on for a member at the member rate. `non_member` = standard rate (default for everyone else).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code. Hardcoded `USD` in V1.

amount.description: Total of the selected option (`is_selected = true`). `null` when no option is selected yet.

signed_total.description: Customer-signed all-in total snapshotted at approval — base items + the add-ons the customer accepted + discounts + tax. The legally-binding number the customer signed for, and the source of truth the auto-created job's invoice must match. `null` until the estimate is approved. Differs from `amount` (selected option's base price) only when the customer accepted add-on line items.

options.description: Good / Better / Best tiers sorted by `sort_order`. Empty array when the estimate has no options yet.

property_id.description: Service-location FK. Set by the QBO importer for invoices that originally belonged to a QBO sub-customer (so per-location history survives flattening sub-customers into properties). Null for native invoices today.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code. Hardcoded `USD` in V1.

examples: Service call + evaporator coil replacement. Net 30.

membership_id.description: Phase 21 (M1.a): the membership this invoice renews, when `invoice_type === 'membership_renewal'`. Null on job-derived (`'manual'`) invoices.

invoice_type.description: Phase 21 (M1.a): `'manual'` = job-derived (historical default). `'membership_renewal'` = auto-generated by the daily membership renewal cron. `'milestone'` = a payment-schedule milestone invoice — one of several invoices billing the same job's estimate schedule.

milestone_id.description: The estimate payment milestone this invoice bills, when `invoice_type === 'milestone'`. A job with a payment schedule produces one invoice per milestone, each with its own status, due date, and pay link. Null on non-milestone invoices.

status.description: Membership lifecycle state. `active` = currently paid and auto-renewing. `non_renewing` = customer cancelled — benefits + visits remain through end_date, no future billing cycles. `suspended` = paused by the org — billing paused, benefits paused, clock resumes on reactivate. `cancelled` = ended immediately (admin override). `expired` = end_date passed without renewal (terminal).

examples: Customer moved out of service area.

last_renewed_at.description: Phase 21 (M1.b): timestamp of the most recent successful renewal rollover. `null` until the first renewal invoice is paid. The `membership.renewed` webhook fires once per rollover.

units.description: M5.a: number of equipment units this membership covers. Default 1 (matches pre-M5.a flat-pricing semantics). When the linked plan has `price_per_unit` configured, the MRR is per-unit: `price_per_unit + (units - 1) × price_each_additional`, capped at the plan's `max_units`. Otherwise the membership's flat plan price applies regardless of units.

autopay_enabled.description: Phase 21 (M1.f): true when the member has opted in to off-session Stripe Connect autopay AND a vaulted PaymentMethod is on file for the org's Connect account. Read-only on this surface — turning autopay on requires Stripe-side card tokenization done in the in-app settings flow, not via REST.

EquipmentSystemPatchRequest.description: Partial update. At least one field must be present. Unknown keys return 422. Systems are property-scoped so `property_id` cannot be reassigned — soft-delete and re-create instead.

JobWriteRequest.description: Required FKs (customer, property) can be supplied as UUID OR as `{name}_external_id` + `{name}_external_source`. Equipment is optional, same dual form. PATCH on jobs is status-only.

JobPatchRequest.description: Status-only per spec §4. Invalid transitions (per VALID_STATUS_TRANSITIONS — same rules the UI enforces) return 422. No-op (same status) returns 200 with the unchanged row.

sequence.description: 1-based ordinal within the parent job. Server-assigned (max+1) when omitted; provide explicitly only to insert before existing appointments (advanced).

examples: Pull permit before 9am — Building Dept opens at 8.

assignments.description: Optional inline crew. At most one Lead per appointment (the partial unique index enforces it). When you need to promote a Helper to Lead later, PATCH /assignments/{tech_id} — that auto-demotes the previous Lead.

AppointmentWriteRequest.description: Add a day to a multi-day install. Body shape is intentionally narrow — appointment status starts at 'scheduled' (transition via PATCH), `customer_notified_at` is server-stamped on Lead actions, `actual_*` are field-side outputs.

AppointmentPatchRequest.description: All fields optional. Status changes follow the forward-only transition table — invalid transitions return 422. Empty body is a no-op (returns the row as-is).

scheduled_start.description: Optional per-tech window override; falls back to the appointment-level window when null.

AssignmentWriteRequest.description: Add a tech to an appointment with a role. POSTing role='lead' to an appointment that already has a Lead returns 409 — PATCH the new tech's assignment to promote (auto-demotes the previous Lead).

AssignmentPatchRequest.description: Change a tech's role on an appointment or update their per-tech window override. Promoting to Lead auto-demotes the previous Lead. `tech_state` / clock fields are mobile-app-driven, not exposed for write.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor. Pass back as `?starting_after=<cursor>` to fetch the next page.

service_agreement_id.description: Service agreement UUID. The service_agreements table does NOT carry external_id columns in V1 — UUID is the only supported reference.

to_customer_id.description: UUID of the new homeowner. Must belong to the same organization.

reason.description: Optional dispatcher-typed note recorded on the audit row. Example: 'house sold to John Smith Mar 2026'.

MembershipTransferRequest.description: House-sale transfer body. Sends the membership to a new homeowner with audit trail. `customer_id` is the only field that changes.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code. Hardcoded `USD` in V1.

track_inventory.description: When true, this item's stock is tracked. Automatically true for `type = part` items in orgs that have enabled inventory tracking; false otherwise. Read-only from the API — flip via the /settings/inventory UI.

quantity_on_hand.description: Current stock level. Denormalized from stock_movements; always fresh. Zero when inventory is disabled at the org or when tracking has never been enabled for this item.

reorder_point.description: Low-stock threshold. When quantity_on_hand drops to or below this value, the item appears in the /inventory low-stock banner. Null = no threshold set = never surfaced as low.

weighted_avg_cost.description: Running weighted-average cost basis, recomputed on each PO receive. Null until the first receive or until the org toggles inventory on with a seeded unit_cost. Used to snapshot COGS at consume time.

examples: Hi Jenny, this is Acme HVAC. Your tech is on the way — ETA 9:15am.

JobTagsRequest.description: Set the exact tag list on a job (idempotent reconcile — tags in the body are added, tags not in the body are detached). Fires `job.tagged` webhook per newly-attached tag.

amount.description: Payment amount in major units (e.g. dollars).

collected_at.description: Defaults to now in the org's timezone.

PaymentCreateRequest.description: Record a payment against an invoice. Recomputes invoice status (paid / partial / unchanged) from sum of succeeded payments. If the payment closes the invoice, parent job status moves to `closed` (when it was at `invoiced`). Always fires `payment.received` webhook; fires `invoice.paid` when the invoice newly hits full payment.

200.description: OK — an existing row was updated. Returned when the supplied `external_id` + `external_source` matched an existing row (idempotent retry).

201.description: Created — a new row was inserted.

400.description: Invalid request — query parameter validation failed.

404.description: Resource not found (or exists in another org — existence is masked).

409.description: Concurrent INSERT race lost — another request with the same `external_id` + `external_source` won. Retry the same payload; the upsert will hit the existing row and return 200.

422.description: Request body validation failed (Zod). Common causes: missing required field, unknown field (.strict()), `external_id` without `external_source`, FK reference not found in this organization, invalid job status transition.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the seconds in `Retry-After`.
